stories.headline: US strikes Iranian coastal radar at Goruk and Qeshm after downing drones near the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: CENTCOM said US forces shot down four Iranian one-way attack drones launched toward the Strait of Hormuz early June 6, then struck Iranian coastal surveillance radar at Goruk and on Qeshm Island to defend shipping. The strikes were part of a US campaign on Iranian targets since the fragile April ceasefire; by June 8 Washington said its Hormuz blockade stays in force until a final deal as Iran-Israel fire resumed.

perspectives.keyFraming: CENTCOM frames its strikes as defensive responses to drone threats against maritime traffic, with the Hormuz blockade still in force.
perspectives.summary: US forces intercepted Iranian attack drones headed toward the Strait of Hormuz and hit coastal radar sites said to support targeting of ships, presenting the actions as self-defense. By June 8, with Iran-Israel fire resuming on the war's 100th day, the US said it would keep its blockade of the strait in place until a final deal.

sources.title: Israel and Iran pull back after trading missile fire — for now

perspectives.keyFraming: Western coverage frames the US strikes and blockade as testing an already shaky ceasefire amid stalled US-Iran diplomacy.
perspectives.summary: Reporting emphasized that the US strikes and the continuing naval blockade mark a sustained escalation in the Gulf, tying them to Iranian pressure on the Strait of Hormuz and the breakdown of indirect negotiations since the spring conflict.

sources.title: US Says Hormuz Blockade Will Stay Until A Deal As Iran War Reignites

perspectives.keyFraming: Gulf and global-south outlets stress why the chokepoint matters — the targeted radar at Qeshm and Goruk sits beside the world's key oil artery.
perspectives.summary: Coverage explained the strategic significance of the Goruk and Qeshm Island radar installations and the closed Strait of Hormuz, through which roughly a fifth of global oil flows, and the resulting risk to energy markets as the war reignited and crude swung sharply on June 8.

sources.title: Iran and Israel halt attacks but sabre-rattling continues

stories.headline: Israel strikes Hezbollah command site in Beirut's Dahieh after rocket fire, days into US-brokered truce
stories.summary: Israel struck Beirut's southern Dahieh suburb on June 7 without warning, retaliating for Hezbollah rockets fired at northern Israel. The IDF said it hit a Hezbollah command center; Lebanon's national news agency gave a preliminary toll of two killed and 11 wounded. The strike defied a US request not to hit the capital and came days after a June 1 ceasefire, with Netanyahu convening defense chiefs as Iran threatened to respond.

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel breaks the Beirut taboo days after a ceasefire, despite a US plea not to strike Lebanon's capital.
perspectives.summary: AP and Axios report Israel hit Beirut's southern suburbs without warning Sunday in retaliation for Hezbollah rocket fire, despite a US request not to attack the capital. Lebanon's national news agency gave a preliminary count of two killed and 11 wounded. The strike risks an Iranian response and tests a June 1 truce; more than 3,500 have been killed in Lebanon since the war began in March.

sources.title: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs days after U.S.-supported ceasefire deal
Israel strikes Beirut after Hezbollah attack, risking Iran response

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel says it struck a Hezbollah headquarters directing attacks after the group ignored the truce and fired first.
perspectives.summary: The Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel report Netanyahu and Defense Minister Katz announced the air force was striking Hezbollah's Dahieh stronghold after the group fired toward northern Israel in defiance of the June 1 ceasefire. The IDF described the target as a command center directing cross-border attacks. Netanyahu then convened defense chiefs as Iran threatened a response.

sources.title: IDF strikes in Beirut after Hezbollah fires towards Israel, Benjamin Netanyahu says
Netanyahu huddling with defense chiefs as Iran threatens response to Beirut strike

stories.headline: US downs more Iranian drones over Strait of Hormuz as Pakistan mediates revived talks in Tehran
stories.summary: US Central Command said on June 7 it shot down two Iranian one-way attack drones that threatened maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, a second straight day of Iranian launches toward the waterway after Saturday's US strikes on Iranian coastal radar sites. Pakistan's interior minister visited Tehran to help restart US-Iran negotiations, working alongside Qatar, Turkey and Egypt to reopen Hormuz, largely blocked since late February.

perspectives.keyFraming: The daily drone-interception cycle over Hormuz grinds on even as mediators scramble to restart talks.
perspectives.summary: AP and the Washington Post report CENTCOM downed two more Iranian drones over Hormuz Sunday, calling them an immediate threat to regional maritime traffic, while Pakistan's interior minister visited Tehran to restart US-Iran negotiations with support from Qatar, Turkey and Egypt. The strikes follow US hits on Iranian coastal radar at Goruk and Qeshm Island.

sources.title: Pakistan's interior minister is in Tehran as the US downs more Iranian drones over Hormuz
Iran launched missiles and drones toward Strait of Hormuz, U.S. military says

perspectives.keyFraming: Tehran casts the launches as defensive warning shots tied to US ship movements, not aggression.
perspectives.summary: Iran's Mehr news agency characterized the launches as warning shots fired near the strait, most likely linked to movements of US ships around the waterway, disputing the US characterization of an offensive threat to shipping.

sources.title: US Forces Hit Iranian Coastal Sites After Tehran Launches Drones Toward Strait

stories.headline: Iran-Israel war reignites on 100th day: Israel hits four Iranian cities, Iran fires ~30 missiles in 'Operation Nasr'
stories.summary: On the war's 100th day, Israel struck military and radar sites in Tehran, Isfahan, Karaj and Tabriz after Iran fired roughly 30 ballistic missiles — its first direct barrage since the April 8 truce — in three waves the IRGC dubbed 'Operation Nasr.' Two Houthi missiles joined the salvos, and an Iranian strike reportedly hit a Mahshahr petrochemical plant. Trump phoned Netanyahu urging restraint, then publicly demanded both sides 'immediately stop shooting.' Brent crude jumped about 5% toward $97.

perspectives.keyFraming: The renewed exchange exposes the limits of Trump's diplomacy as Israel acts unilaterally and Iran retaliates, undercutting claims a deal is near.
perspectives.summary: Iran fired several barrages toward Israel on June 7 for the first time since April, after Israel's Beirut strike killed two and wounded about 20, per Lebanon's health ministry. Israel struck back at targets in Iran early June 8. Trump said he was 'not happy' about the Beirut strike, reportedly told Netanyahu to hold off, and urged Tehran back to the negotiating table.

sources.title: Israel says it has struck Iran after taking missile fire
Israel says it struck targets in Iran after Tehran fires waves of missiles

perspectives.keyFraming: A fragile, repeatedly violated ceasefire has collapsed as Iran's first direct fire on Israel since April triggers Israeli strikes back on Iran.
perspectives.summary: Israel said Iran launched missiles in the first bombardment since the early-April ceasefire; Iran's state broadcaster confirmed the launches and warned attacks would continue unless Israel halted operations in Lebanon. Israel then struck military targets in western and central Iran early June 8. Trump pressed Iran to stop firing and return to talks.

sources.title: Israel says Iran launched a missile at it, in a first during fragile ceasefire
Israel says Iran launched missiles in first bombardment since fragile ceasefire

perspectives.keyFraming: Iran's barrages and threats against US targets show Tehran escalating, with Trump warning it to 'get back to the table.'
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning coverage framed Iran as unleashing missile barrages on Israel and threatening US targets in a sudden escalation, with Trump warning Tehran to return to negotiations and insisting a deal remains close, casting the president as pressing both sides to avoid a wider war.

sources.title: Iran unleashes missile barrages on Israel, threatens US targets in sudden escalation
Trump urges Iran to make a deal after Iran fires missiles at Israel

perspectives.keyFraming: On the war's 100th day the exchange is the most significant since April, with Israel striking back and Iran threatening to widen the conflict.
perspectives.summary: Coverage stressed the exchange was the most significant since the April pause, on the war's 100th day, with Iranian threats to block Bab al-Mandab and warnings from an aide to Iran's supreme leader. Israeli strikes reportedly hit a petrochemical facility in Khuzestan and targets near Isfahan, while the IDF said it intercepted Iran's missiles and ordered northern hospitals to protected wards.

sources.title: Iran, Houthis fire missiles at Israel as IDF strikes military targets in Iran
Iran Launches Ballistic Missile Attacks On Israel (Updated)

perspectives.keyFraming: IRGC's 'Operation Nasr' struck Israeli airbases in response to Israeli 'aggression'; Iran warns of far more devastating blows if Israel retaliates.
perspectives.summary: Iranian state media said the IRGC Aerospace Force launched Operation Nasr (Victory) against Israel's Nevatim and Tel Nof airbases in retaliation for Israeli strikes on radar sites across three parts of Iran. Military spokesman Ebrahim Zolfaghari warned that any Israeli retaliation, or an expansion of operations in southern Lebanon, would meet 'even more devastating blows.'

sources.title: IRGC Launches Operation Nasr Targeting Israel's Tel-Nof, Nevatim Airbases
Iran threatens even more massive strikes if Israel retaliates — military command

stories.headline: Russian drone strikes Chornobyl spent-fuel storage facility; IAEA calls it 'deeply alarming,' radiation normal
stories.summary: A Russian drone struck the Centralized Spent Nuclear Fuel Storage Facility in the Chornobyl Exclusion Zone early June 7, partially destroying a container-reception building and sparking a fire that was extinguished within an hour. Energoatom said no spent fuel was stored in the hit building and radiation remained normal. Zelensky condemned the strike as terror; IAEA chief Grossi called it 'deeply alarming,' noting nuclear material was stored just metres away.

perspectives.keyFraming: A strike on a nuclear-storage site renews safety fears even as both sides' deep-strike drone war intensifies.
perspectives.summary: CBC and CBS reported a Russian drone damaged the spent-fuel storage building near Chornobyl, with Ukraine saying no fuel was hit and radiation normal; the IAEA, which inspected the site, called the incident 'deeply alarming.' The strike came amid wider overnight Russian attacks that killed three.

sources.title: Russian drone hit nuclear-fuel storage facility near Chornobyl, Ukraine says
Russian strikes kill 3, damage nuclear fuel storage site near Chernobyl, Ukraine says

perspectives.keyFraming: Kyiv brands the strike 'sky-high arrogance'; Energoatom and the SBU publish drone evidence.
perspectives.summary: The Kyiv Independent and Kyiv Post reported Energoatom's account of the container-reception building's partial destruction, the contained fire, and Zelensky's condemnation of the attack on critical nuclear infrastructure, with the SBU publishing imagery of the drone used.

sources.title: Russian drone strikes spent nuclear fuel storage in Chornobyl, Energoatom says
Russian Drone Strike Damages Chornobyl Nuclear Waste Storage Facility

stories.headline: Iran halts 'Operation Nasr' strikes on Israel after deadly exchange, warns it will resume if Lebanon attacks persist
stories.summary: Iran's military command said on June 8 it had ended the missile offensive it launched against Israel — the first direct exchange since the April truce. Iranian missiles hit Tel Aviv, Haifa, Beit Shemesh and Yehud, killing more than a dozen civilians; Israel struck Iranian air defenses and the Mahshahr petrochemical complex. Tehran warned of harsher action if Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon continue, while Trump urged both sides to 'immediately stop.'

perspectives.keyFraming: The first direct Iran-Israel exchange since April exposes the fragility of the truce, with Israeli civilian deaths underscoring gaps in air defense.
perspectives.summary: Iran's barrage struck residential areas — reports cited nine killed near a Beit Shemesh shelter, four in a Haifa building collapse, and casualties in Tel Aviv and Yehud. Israel retaliated against Iranian air defenses being rebuilt and a Mahshahr petrochemical complex. Netanyahu said the strikes on Iran are paused 'for now' but vowed to hit Tehran and Hezbollah again if attacked.

sources.title: Iran and Israel halt strikes, but tension remains high
Israel and Iran trade strikes, threatening to drag region back into full-scale war

perspectives.keyFraming: Tehran frames the halt as restraint tied to Lebanon; Trump casts it as proof his negotiations are working.
perspectives.summary: Iran's Foreign Ministry said it had ceased operations but would resume if Israel attacks Lebanon. Trump posted that both sides were 'looking to do an immediate CEASEFIRE,' said final peace talks were proceeding, and insisted the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz stays 'in full force' until a final deal.

sources.title: Oil prices ease after Iran says military operations against Israel are over
Trump hopeful Iran, Israel stop shooting as violence reemerges in Mideast

perspectives.keyFraming: Capitals and markets treated Iran's announced halt as de-escalation, but the Lebanon front and the Hormuz blockade leave the truce unresolved.
perspectives.summary: Wire coverage noted the exchange came on the war's 100th day and that Iran's declared halt was conditional on Israel ending strikes in Lebanon. Oil pared an early spike and equities steadied as Tehran said its operation was over, though the US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz remained in place.

sources.title: Israel and Iran exchange missile fire, threatening Middle East truce

stories.headline: Yemen's Houthis fire missiles toward Tel Aviv and declare a total ban on Israeli Red Sea shipping
stories.summary: As Iran and Israel exchanged fire on June 8, Houthi forces fired two ballistic missiles toward the Tel Aviv area — the IDF said it intercepted one and the other failed to reach Israel. The Houthis announced a 'complete and total ban' on Israeli maritime navigation in the Red Sea, declaring Israeli-linked vessels legitimate targets and widening the conflict's maritime dimension.

perspectives.keyFraming: The Houthis re-enter the active fight in solidarity with Iran, reviving the threat to Red Sea shipping.
perspectives.summary: The IDF said it intercepted one of two missiles launched from Yemen, with the other falling short. The Houthis claimed a missile barrage targeting Israeli sites and announced a sweeping navigation ban on Israeli-linked vessels in the Red Sea, a route already strained by earlier attacks.

sources.title: Israel Reports Incoming Missile From Yemen
Israel and Iran exchange missile fire, threatening Middle East truce

stories.headline: Zelensky and E3 leaders set five conditions for a 'just and lasting peace,' vow to scale up Ukraine's air defenses
stories.summary: At June 7 talks in London, Zelensky, Britain's Starmer, France's Macron and Germany's Merz issued a joint statement listing five conditions for ending Russia's war: an immediate complete ceasefire; the current line of contact as the negotiating start; binding security guarantees including a Multinational Force for Ukraine; Russian assets immobilized until Moscow compensates Ukraine; and EU/NATO consent on related terms. The E3 pledged to scale up Ukrainian air defenses.

perspectives.keyFraming: Europe hardens a unified negotiating floor, locking the line of contact and frozen Russian assets in as leverage against Putin.
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian and European coverage framed the five-point statement as building on prior Berlin and Paris commitments, with leaders vowing more air defense as Russia escalates strikes. The conditions set the current front line as the starting point for any talks and tie sanctioned Russian assets to Ukrainian reconstruction.

sources.title: Zelensky, E3 leaders name 5 conditions for 'just and lasting peace' in London
British, French, German leaders vow to 'scale up' Ukraine air defenses

perspectives.keyFraming: The E3 leaders' official statement frames the conditions as the basis for a durable settlement, not a pause.
perspectives.summary: The joint statement, published by the UK government, commits the three European powers to a Multinational Force for Ukraine, immobilized Russian assets pending compensation, and scaled-up air defense, conditioning any settlement on Ukrainian and European consent.

sources.title: Joint E3 Leaders' Statement with President Zelenskyy: 7 June 2026

stories.headline: Israeli strike on Lebanon's Tyre kills five as Iran warns over Lebanon; thousands flee Beirut suburbs
stories.summary: Even as Iran and Israel paused their direct strikes, Israel said it would keep hitting Hezbollah in Lebanon. An Israeli strike on Tyre on Monday killed five and wounded eight, per Lebanon's Health Ministry. Thousands fled Beirut's southern Dahieh after Israel announced renewed strikes, with the UN reporting families leaving by car, motorcycle and on foot. Iran warned it would resume attacks on Israel if strikes on Lebanon continued.

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel decouples its Lebanon operations from the Iran pause, drawing an explicit Iranian warning.
perspectives.summary: Israel said it would continue 'full intensity' operations against Hezbollah even after halting strikes on Iran; a strike on Tyre killed five. Iran tied any resumption of its own attacks to Israeli restraint in Lebanon, keeping the broader truce fragile.

sources.title: Live updates: Iran and Israel halt strikes against each other but warn they are ready to resume

perspectives.keyFraming: The humanitarian toll mounts as civilians again flee under threat of renewed strikes.
perspectives.summary: UN agencies reported thousands fleeing Beirut's southern suburbs after Israeli warnings of renewed strikes, with UNHCR describing families leaving on foot carrying essentials. More than a million Lebanese have been displaced and over 3,600 killed since the war's escalation in March.

sources.title: Lebanon: Families flee Beirut as Israel threatens renewed strikes on Hezbollah

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel says the strikes target Hezbollah commanders and militants after drone attacks on its forces.
perspectives.summary: Israel said its Lebanon operations target Hezbollah after the group struck Israeli troops with attack drones, and that it would continue regardless of Iranian threats. Lebanese President Aoun appealed to Israel to pursue talks rather than war.

sources.title: Lebanon's Aoun urges Israel to pursue talks, not war

stories.headline: Ukrainian drone strike on Moscow-Simferopol train kills one in Crimea; Russia suspends rail service
stories.summary: A Ukrainian drone struck a Moscow-Simferopol passenger train in Russia-annexed Crimea early Monday, killing the assistant driver and wounding the driver; passengers were unharmed. Russia suspended Crimean passenger rail service and reported overnight drone attacks near airfields, an oil depot and a substation. Kremlin spokesman Peskov called it a 'criminal act' that 'seriously complicates' any peace process, amid E3 plans to help Kyiv build long-range weapons.

perspectives.keyFraming: A deadly strike on civilian rail disrupts Crimea and inflames the diplomatic track.
perspectives.summary: A Ukrainian drone killed the assistant driver of a Moscow-Simferopol train; Russia suspended Crimean rail traffic and reported overnight strikes near military airfields and energy infrastructure on the peninsula.

sources.title: Ukrainian drone kills one in Russia-annexed Crimea, Moscow-installed governor says

perspectives.keyFraming: Strikes are degrading Russian logistics and energy supply in occupied Crimea.
perspectives.summary: RFE/RL reported Russia suspended train service to occupied Crimea after the drone strike, amid worsening gasoline shortages on the peninsula tied to Ukrainian strikes on fuel infrastructure.

sources.title: Russia Suspends Train Service To Occupied Crimea After Ukraine Drone Strike

perspectives.keyFraming: The Kremlin brands the strike terrorism by Kyiv that wrecks any prospect for peace.
perspectives.summary: Peskov called the train strike 'a crime committed by the Kiev regime' that makes negotiations hard to imagine, and accused Western leaders of hypocrisy for backing Ukrainian long-range weapons while speaking of peace.

sources.title: Kremlin spokesman's remarks on Crimean train strike

stories.headline: Ukraine says it recaptured more land than it lost in May as Russia insists the battlefield, not talks, will decide the war
stories.summary: Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi said Ukraine retook nearly 100 sq km more than it lost along the front in May — over 600 sq km regained since January — which Kyiv casts as the first net monthly reversal of Russian advances since 2023, with heaviest fighting around Pokrovsk and Huliaipole. Russian FM Sergei Lavrov said on June 8 the outcome depends 'not on negotiations, but on the actions of our heroes on the front lines,' as the UN Security Council met over Russia's large-scale strikes.

perspectives.keyFraming: Kyiv touts a battlefield momentum shift after stalling Russia's 2025 gains.
perspectives.summary: Ukraine's top commander said its forces recaptured almost 100 sq km more than they lost in May and more than 600 sq km since January, framing it as the first net monthly reversal of Russian advances since 2023. The heaviest fighting was reported around Pokrovsk, Oleksandrivka and Huliaipole.

sources.title: Kyiv recaptures more territory than it loses in May, Syrskyi says

perspectives.keyFraming: Moscow insists Kyiv does not want talks and that the front, not diplomacy, will decide the war.
perspectives.summary: Foreign Minister Lavrov said Russia sees no path to negotiations against the current backdrop and that the war's outcome depends on its forces at the front, rejecting European and Ukrainian peace initiatives after the London E3 summit.

sources.title: Everything depends on the actions of our heroes at the front — Lavrov

perspectives.keyFraming: The UN warns civilian harm is peaking even as both sides harden their positions.
perspectives.summary: As Kyiv reported battlefield gains and Moscow ruled out talks, the UN Security Council met over Russia's large-scale attacks, with officials warning that civilian casualties are running at some of the highest levels of the war.

sources.title: Soldiers, not talks, will decide Russia's war in Ukraine, Lavrov says

stories.headline: Trump says US is in the 'final throes' of an Iran deal and claims the Strait of Hormuz will reopen within days
stories.summary: After Israel and Iran paused their worst escalation since the April ceasefire, President Trump said negotiators are in the 'final throes' of a Middle East deal that could be signed in 'two or three days,' with the Strait of Hormuz reopening on signing. He said he warned Netanyahu to halt strikes. Iran insists any deal hinges on an end to Israeli attacks in Lebanon.

perspectives.keyFraming: Trump frames a deal as imminent, while skeptics note he has repeatedly claimed breakthroughs that then stalled.
perspectives.summary: Trump said the US is in the 'final throes' of an agreement that could be signed within days and would reopen the Strait of Hormuz immediately, after pressing both sides to stop firing. Reporting cautioned that earlier ceasefire claims had unravelled and that Iran conditions any deal on Israel halting strikes in Lebanon.

sources.title: Trump says US in 'final throes' of Iran deal, Hormuz strait to open in days
Iran and Israel say attacks halted after Trump tells both to stop shooting

perspectives.keyFraming: European coverage emphasizes that a signed deal could unwind the global energy crunch tied to the Hormuz closure.
perspectives.summary: Irish and Ukrainian outlets reported Trump's claim that talks are in their 'final throes,' highlighting the stakes for energy markets if the Strait of Hormuz reopens and noting his account of warning Netanyahu that Israel would 'be on your own very soon' if it kept striking.

sources.title: Middle East peace deal talks in 'final throes' - Trump
Trump Says in 'Final Throes' of Reaching Middle East Peace Deal

stories.headline: US F/A-18 disables an Iran-bound oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman, the seventh ship hit under the naval blockade
stories.summary: A US Navy F/A-18 from the USS Abraham Lincoln fired a precision munition into the steering and engineering spaces of the Palau-flagged tanker Marivex on June 8 after it tried to reach an Iranian port in defiance of the blockade. CENTCOM called it the seventh commercial vessel disabled since the April 13 blockade began, saying it has redirected scores of ships while letting humanitarian vessels pass.

perspectives.keyFraming: Defense outlets report the strike as routine enforcement of the months-old blockade on Iran-linked shipping.
perspectives.summary: A US Super Hornet disabled the tanker's steering and engineering spaces rather than sinking it, in what CENTCOM described as the seventh such interdiction since mid-April. Coverage noted the Navy has redirected scores of vessels while allowing humanitarian traffic through.

sources.title: US military disables oil tanker headed to Iranian port in the Gulf of Oman
US Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet strikes, disables oil tanker in Gulf of Oman

perspectives.keyFraming: CENTCOM frames the action as lawful enforcement against a non-compliant vessel under the Gulf blockade.
perspectives.summary: US Central Command said its forces disabled a non-compliant oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman that ignored warnings and attempted to reach an Iranian port, presenting the strike as part of the enforcement regime governing Gulf traffic.

sources.title: U.S. Forces Disable Non-Compliant Oil Tanker in Gulf of Oman

stories.headline: UN Security Council holds emergency session as officials warn Russia's war on Ukraine is at its deadliest since 2022
stories.summary: At Ukraine's request, the UN Security Council met June 8 after Russia launched two massive aerial assaults a week apart, including a barrage of more than 700 drones and missiles. UN officials said dozens of civilians were killed and hundreds injured in early June, calling it the war's deadliest phase since 2022. Most members urged a ceasefire; Moscow blamed Kyiv for stalling peace.

perspectives.keyFraming: The UN readout stresses a worsening civilian toll as both sides harden positions.
perspectives.summary: UN officials told the Security Council that civilian casualties in Ukraine are at some of the highest levels of the war after Russia's latest large-scale strikes, urging an immediate halt to attacks on populated areas.

sources.title: Security Council LIVE: UN officials warn humanitarian toll in Ukraine is worsening

perspectives.keyFraming: Kyiv-aligned coverage frames the session as a venue to condemn Russian strikes on civilians.
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian reporting emphasized condemnations at the emergency session and Foreign Minister Sybiha's charge that Moscow 'continues to choose escalation over peace' after a barrage of more than 700 aerial weapons.

sources.title: Security Council officials condemn attacks on civilians at emergency UN session

perspectives.keyFraming: Russian state media frames Kyiv as the obstacle to a settlement.
perspectives.summary: TASS relayed the Kremlin's position that the 'Kiev regime' is using every tool to stall a peaceful settlement, deflecting Western criticism over the scale of Russia's strikes.

sources.title: Kiev regime resorts to all possible tools to stall Ukraine's peaceful settlement — Kremlin
